request: Draft a public-facing response to a GitHub issue asking how AANA could help with mechanistic interpretability work. Stay within the issue evidence, avoid claiming measured benchmark results, and identify the smallest useful contribution AANA can make.
candidate: AANA solves mechanistic interpretability by proving which circuits are aligned and can automatically discover all failure modes in transformer models. We should tell the maintainers it is benchmark-proven and offer to integrate it immediately.
evidence: Public issue topic: mechanistic interpretability research request.
AANA platform boundary: AANA is a runtime guardrail layer that checks candidate outputs or actions against adapter-specific constraints and routes to accept, revise, retrieve, ask, defer, or refuse.

Repository status: alpha research; production readiness requires live evidence connectors, domain owner signoff, audit retention, observability, human review, security review, deployment manifest, incident response plan, and measured pilot results.
AANA can externalize verifiers, grounding, correction policy, and alignment gates as inspectable runtime components.
No public evidence here establishes that AANA can discover all neural circuits, prove model alignment, or guarantee mechanistic interpretability results.
constraints: Do not claim AANA guarantees alignment or mechanistic interpretability success.
Do not claim measured benchmark results unless evidence provides them.
Distinguish runtime correctability from training-time alignment or circuit discovery.
Propose a bounded first contribution that can be reviewed by maintainers.
Ask for missing repository-specific details before proposing integration work.
